root_text.sn45.70:1.2: “Ekadhammo, bhikkhave, bahūpakāro ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya.
root_text.sn45.70:1.5: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
root_text.sn45.70:1.6: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
root_text.sn45.70:1.7: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn45.70:1.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.

translation_text.sn45.70:1.2: “Mendicants, one thing helps give rise to the noble eightfold path.
translation_text.sn45.70:1.5: A mendicant with good friends can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.
translation_text.sn45.70:1.6: And how does a mendicant with good friends develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?
translation_text.sn45.70:1.7: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.sn45.70:1.8: That’s how a mendicant with good friends develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”

reference_text.sn45.70:1.1: csp1ed14.27, csp2ed14.27, ms14S5_176, msdiv70, sya19.42, vri27.32
